data.title: Indonesia court shortens investor land rights by half in fresh blow to capital city project
data.description:
Indonesia's Constitutional Court has capped the maximum duration of land rights for investors in its planned new capital city at 95 years, half the previous level, according to a new ruling, dealing another blow to the ambitious $32 billion project.

Former President Joko Widodo in 2019 unveiled plans to move Indonesia's capital from the overcrowded and subsiding Jakarta on Java island to Nusantara, a new city now being built in the Borneo jungle, but construction has been delayed by the COVID-19 pandemic.

The viability of the project has also been thrown into doubt after the project's budget was cut significantly by Prabowo Subianto, who took over the presidency last year, though he still pledged to turn Nusantara into the Southeast Asian country's political capital in the coming years.

A panel of Constitutional Court justices issued the ruling on Thursday after a petition filed by two Borneo locals, according to state news agency Antara.

The petitioners had argued that granting land rights for an excessively long duration could compromise the interests of future generations, according to court website.

Widodo intended to rely on investors to fund the construction of Nusantara, offering incentives like tax breaks and lengthy land rights of up to 190 years, far longer than elsewhere in the country.

A number of Indonesian firms have declared interest to invest in Nusantara, but there has been little interest shown so far by foreign companies.

The Constitutional Court's ruling is final and binding.

data.title: Yomiuri: Japan's Land Ministry to Lower Size Requirement for Mortgage Tax Break
data.description:
By Rui Suzuki / Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

The Japanese land ministry has begun discussions on revising the guideline for homes that qualify for a mortgage tax break, aiming to lower the minimum size requirement from the current 50 square meters to around 40 square meters, sources said. The change is intended to allow more buyers to utilize the tax break, as smaller homes with lower sales prices become more common amid the surge in housing prices.

The Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Ministry plans to include the revision in the basic plan for housing, which is expected to be finalized within fiscal 2025. The current threshold of 50 square meters is based on a household consisting of parents and two children. There is an exception for newly built homes, which allows a minimum of 40 square meters if the household's total income is 10 million yen or less.

However, with the recent increase in single-person and elderly couple households, housing units with smaller floor areas are also increasing in order to lower the sales price per unit. Therefore, the ministry intends to broaden the scope of eligibility to help reduce the financial burden on households.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-M0000157912-1

data.title: INFLUENCER'S IN-LAWS PLEAD NOT GUILTY TO CAUSING HURT
data.description:
KLANG, Nov 14 (Bernama) — The parents and brothers-in-law of social media influencer Syed Muhammad Shah Hasif Syed Othman, better known as Prince Syed, were charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with committing mischief and voluntarily causing hurt to him.

Ishak Saif, 59, Aminah Haniffa, 55, Muhammad Syahmi Faiz Ishak, 31, and Muhammad Ikhwan Alif Ishak, 22, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read to them before Magistrate Siti Zubaidah Mahat.

Ishak, Aminah and Muhammad Ikhwan Alif were jointly charged with voluntarily causing hurt to Syed Muhammad Shah Hasif, 33, at a residential area in Sungai Bertek, Klang, on Nov 2.

The charge, under Section 323 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same code, provides for up to one year in prison, a fine of up to RM2,000, or both, upon conviction.

Muhammad Syahmi Faiz was accused of committing mischief by breaking the window of a sports utility vehicle (SUV) belonging to the influencer’s wife, Fatin Syazwani Ishak, 27, causing RM560 in losses at the same location and date.

‎-- MORE

‎

‎‎COURT-INFLUENCER 2 (LAST) SHAH ALAM

‎

The charge was framed under Section 427 of the Penal Code, which provides for imprisonment of up to five years, a fine, or both, upon conviction.

The court set bail at RM2,000 for Muhammad Syahmi Faiz and ordered him to report to the nearest police station once a month and refrain from approaching or disturbing the victim or prosecution witnesses, while the other three accused were granted bail of RM1,500 each.

Mention of the case was fixed for Jan 26 next year.

Deputy Public Prosecutor Salman Alfarisi Nawawi appeared for the prosecution while all of the accused were represented by counsel K. Nathiya.

‎-- BERNAMA

‎TAGS: court, influencer, hurting mischief, in-laws

data.title: Acciona Energia drops after guidance cut
data.description:
** Shares in renewable energy company Acciona Energia fall around 8% after it lowered its annual target for EBITDA from operations, dragging its parent company Acciona

** Acciona Energia cut its EBITDA from operations target of around 1 bln euros by about 5% due to lower expected production for the year

** J.P.Morgan says in a note it expects the shares to be lower on the guidance downgrade

** It also flags a slow execution of the asset disposals implying higher net debt temporarily, which it says suggests "that 2026 should be another weak year"

** Acciona's shares fall around 6%

data.title: Diverging wind trends set to sway French and German power prices on Monday
data.description:
French and German power prices are expected to follow diverging trends on Monday, as higher wind generation should pressure German spot prices while rising demand coupled with lower wind supply is expected to lift French day-ahead contracts.

The German and French Monday baseload power contracts were untraded by 0856 GMT on Friday, data compiled by LSEG showed. <TRDEBD3>, <TRFRBD3>

The European power market shows a bearish signal on Monday, driven by strong wind generation, notably in Germany and Austria, said LSEG analyst Xiulan He.

German wind power output is expected to jump by 6.2 gigawatts to 20.5 GW on Monday while French wind power generation is projected to shed 5.1 GW to 7.4 GW, data compiled by LSEG showed.

German solar power supply is forecast to dip by 1.3 GW to 2.7 GW.

Power consumption in Germany is forecast to rise by 430 MW to 59.6 GW on Monday despite a 6.4C decrease in average temperatures, while demand in France is expected to jump by 7.3 GW to 56 GW, as temperatures drop by -6.3C.

France is the only country in the region recording a significant increase in consumption, with lower wind generation leading to an increase in residual load on Monday, said Xiulan He.

Dutch and British gas prices edged higher on Friday morning after hitting an 18-month low the previous day, as forecasts of sharply colder weather next week point to rising demand and increased withdrawals from storage.

French nuclear availability rose six percentage points to 82% of total capacity, as the Bugey 4 power plant came back from an unplanned outage on Thursday.

France's EDF aims to secure 10.6 TWh of extra long-term nuclear power contracts for delivery starting in January 2027 to reduce price volatility, targeting utilities, distributors, and large consumers, with plans to eventually open deals to all European players, it said on Thursday.

German year-ahead baseload TRDEBYZ6 was down 0.2% at 88.31 euros/MWh, while the French equivalents TRFRBYZ6 had not started trading, after closing at 52.31 euros/MWh on Thursday.

In the European carbon market CFI2Zc1 the benchmark contract edged down 0.5% to 80.93 euros a metric ton.

About CAB Payments:

CAB Payments Holdings plc is the holding company for Crown Agents Bank Limited. Regulated in the UK, Crown Agents Bank specialises in FX and cross-border payments for hard-to-reach markets. Its strength of network and deep expertise means it can move money into and out of the world's most complex financial markets. Trusted by a global ecosystem of leading institutions across the public, private and development sectors, Crown Agents Bank's strength lies in its network which connects its clients to underserved geographies, giving them access to 100+ currencies across 700+ currency pairs.

The delivery of fast, transparent and efficient transactions moves money where it's needed. Crown Agents Bank's network offers multiple transaction solutions, delivered via a single API, digital trading platforms, or through bespoke approaches developed by its specialist teams.

Crown Agents Bank is one of the first banks to achieve B Corporation™ status. The Bank was awarded the Platinum Sustainability Rating by EcoVadis in 2025, ranked within the top 1% of 100,000 companies assessed across 160 countries and over 200+ industries. Crown Agents Bank was awarded a Gold EcoVadis rating in 2022, 2023 and 2024.

data.title: European shares fall as US rate cut expectations wane; UK fiscal worries resurface
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 drops 0.9%, banks down nearly 2%
  • Richemont and Siemens Energy see gains
  • Investors await eurozone GDP estimates
  • UK's Reeves ditches income tax hike plans, report says

By Johann M Cherian

European shares fell on Friday, as hawkish commentary from U.S. policymakers dampened expectations for an imminent interest rate cut, even as the benchmark index was set for its strongest weekly performance since late September

The pan-European STOXX 600 dropped 0.9% to 575.22 points, as of 0925 GMT, with banks (.SX7P) down nearly 2%. An index indicating investor nervousness (.V2TX) spiked 1.66 points to a one-week high.

Macro developments in the U.S. have been in the spotlight this week and investors were hopeful that the resumption of data releases would point to a weaker economy and give the Federal Reserve reason to lower borrowing costs in December.

However, those expectations were pared after a growing number of Fed policymakers signalled caution on further easing.

"Given the comments that we've seen in the last day or so, it's unlikely that there's going to be a December cut. But that's not to say there won't be a cut in February or with the next meeting. So I don't think markets should get too as focused as they are on this short-term sequencing," said Michael Field, chief equity strategist at Morningstar.

Expectations for a rate cut coupled with the end to the longest-ever U.S. government shutdown had lifted the STOXX index to hit record highs multiple times this week.

Bucking the day's trend, luxury group Richemont <CFR.S> gained 7.5% and lifted the broader luxury sector (.STXLUXP) after reporting quarterly sales well ahead of forecasts.

Siemens Energy <ENR1n.DE> jumped 11.2% after the German company announced plans to pay its first dividend in four years and raised its mid-term outlook.

Meanwhile, UK stocks CURRENCYCOM:UK100, lost over 1% each, pressured by a spike in gilt yields after a report said Finance Minister Rachel Reeves scrapped plans to raise income tax rates in the upcoming budget, raising questions on plans for balancing public finances.

"There are things they (the government) can do around, you know, tinkering around the edges in terms of capital gains and property taxes and things like this. But ultimately none of those things are big enough to move the needle," said Field.

Later in the day, investors will assess initial estimates to third-quarter gross domestic product for the broader euro zone.

IT provider Bechtle jumped 14.4% after announcing results, while Danish vaccine maker Bavarian Nordic <BAVA.CO> lost 6.5% after chair Luc Debruyne said he will step down.

data.title: Take Five: Clearing the backlog
data.description:
The task of clearing the huge backlog of shutdown-delayed U.S. data begins, the world's most valuable company Nvidia publishes its results and new inflation numbers will keep Europe's central banks on their toes.

Over in Asia, new Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's fiscal policy is taking shape, and Chile holds an election in Latin America where pollsters are predicting another step to the right.

Here's your market week ahead from Dhara Ranasinghe and Amanda Cooper in London, Kevin Buckland in Tokyo and Lewis Krauskopf and Rodrigo Campos in New York.

1/CLEARING THE BACKLOG

U.S. government number crunchers begin the task of shovelling out the backlog of data not released during Washington's unprecedented 43-day shutdown.

In 2013, which was the last shutdown to affect the all-important non-farm payrolls report, the figures came out five days after the government reopened.

Based on that timeline, traders could get the September numbers in the coming days, not least because the original release was planned for October 3, just a couple of days after the shutdown began.

Private data that has been published has suggested the labour market continues to weaken. That supports the case for a December Federal Reserve rate cut. Officials are warning, though, that some data may have been lost forever, meaning the economic fog might take time to clear.

2/AI CATCHING

Nvidia's quarterly report on Wednesday will be a critical test for the high-flying AI trade that has started to make some spluttering noises in recent weeks.

The semiconductor giant became the world's first $5 trillion company last month. It has lost a bit since, but with a staggering 8% weighting in the S&P 500 and major clout in many global indexes, it can easily sway markets on its own.

The AI bellwether's forecasts and the broader industry perspective will have ramifications for the wider tech ecosystem. It is going to either ease or feed those nagging investor concerns that this is already the next big bubble.

3/LEVERS OF POWER

After initially suggesting it would leave monetary policy largely to its central bank, Japan's new government is now signalling a more hands-on approach.

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is looking to loosen the fiscal reins and urging the Bank of Japan to go slow on raising rates, while new Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama has argued inflation is yet to sustainably hit the BoJ's 2% target.

The bank still looks primed for a hike in December, although Governor Kazuo Ueda has been cautious about pulling the trigger. Consumer price data due on November 21 should offer clues, but it could well be the crumpled yen that holds the key.

If its weakness affects politically sensitive food and energy prices, Takaichi may have no choice but to accept some speedy rate hikes.

4/HAPPY PLACE

It must be nice to be the European Central Bank right now. President Christine Lagarde says it is "in a good place" with interest rates and money markets having switched to autopilot, pricing in no move at all next year.

The coming week brings a raft of October inflation numbers, for both individual countries and the euro zone as a whole. Core consumer inflation was 2.4% in September, up from 2.3% in August but down from 2.7% last September.

The headline number has stayed around the ECB's 2% target for most of the year, however, and if the trade-weighted euro's 5.5% 2025 rise starts to drag it lower at any point Frankfurt would have room to cut again.

But the jury is out for now.

5/HOT CHILE

Chile holds the first round of its presidential election on Sunday. Pollsters expect the leftist coalition candidate Jeannette Jara to come out on top, but for the race to then swing to the right in the run-off in a month's time.

Almost as key is the concurrent congressional election, where the right and centre-right are expected to perform strongly. A win in both houses would mark the first such result since the 1950s and would be lapped up by investors who see possible corporate tax cuts if the right also wins the presidency.

The peso has strengthened nearly 7% year-to-date and equities in both dollar and peso terms have soared over 40% - and the rally could easily continue.

Hard-right candidate Jose Antonio Kast and moderate conservative Evelyn Matthei - both market-friendly bets - look most likely to come in second place on Sunday. Yet if far-right libertarian Johannes Kaiser makes it to the runoff against Jara, it could be a different story.

data.title: Macau GDP Growth Confirmed at 8% in Q3
data.description:
Macau’s economy grew by 8% year-on-year in Q2 2025, in line with preliminary estimates from 5.1% in the previous quarter.

Exports of services advanced 10.5%, driven by a 13.6% increase in visitor arrivals, with gains in other tourism services (7.4%) and gaming services (14.3%).

Government spending rose by 2.7%, while consumer spending inched up 0.8%.

Trade also contributed positively to the economy, with goods exports increasing by 4.5% and services exports advancing by 10.5%.

Meanwhile, gross fixed capital formation dropped by 26.1%, reflecting a slowdown in both private and government construction investment projects.

For the first three quarters of 2025, Macau’s GDP expanded by 4.2%.

data.title: DFS Furniture Makes Strong Start to Fiscal 2026 — Market Talk
data.description: DFS Furniture has made an encouraging start to fiscal 2026, Panmure Liberum analysts Wayne Brown and Ben Hunt write. Margins improve due to lower-than-expected freight costs, improved buying and lower interest costs, they write. Orders in the 19 weeks ended Nov. 9 grew compared with the same period a year prior. A continuing focus on innovation, data and technology-enhancing product development is clearly delivering benefits, they write. The company is well positioned to capture latent demand in the U.K. sofa market, they add. Shares trade up 1.6% at 161 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Azerbaijan GDP Grows 1.3% in January to October
data.description:
Azerbaijan’s economy expanded by 1.3% year-on-year in January-October 2025, the same as in the nine months to September, but easing from a 4.9% growth in the same period last year.

The non-oil and gas sector rose by 3.1%, while the oil and gas sector fell by 2.1%.

On the production side, the main contributors to the GDP growth were industry (34%), trade and vehicle repair (10.5%), transportation and warehousing (7.1%), agriculture, forestry, and fishing (6.7%), and construction (6.5%).

data.title: Cidara shares double after report Merck nears buyout deal
data.description:
** Shares of Cidara Therapeutics soar 96% to $208 premarket after a report Merck is close to buying the drugmaker

** Financial Times reports MRK's potential deal values CDTX at a premium to co's $3.3 bln market capitalization

** FT says the buyout deal could be announced as soon as Friday

** MRK was competing with another pharma co before CDTX chose its offer, says FT

** U.S FDA last month, granted a "breakthrough therapy" for CDTX's experimental drug which aimed at preventing influenza A and B in people at high risk of severe illness

** As of last close, CDTX was up 294% year-to-date

data.title: CHINA SEEKS TO ENHANCE AI COOPERATION WITH THAILAND - XI JINPING
data.description:
BEIJING, Nov 14 (Bernama-Sputnik/RIA Novosti) — China is ready to expand cooperation with Thailand in the fields of artificial intelligence (AI) and the aerospace industry, Chinese President Xi Jinping said on Friday, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti.

On Friday, Xi welcomed Thai King Maha Vajiralongkorn (Rama X) in Beijing, who is on a state visit to China from November 13-17.

"China is ready … to expand cooperation in new fields such as artificial intelligence, the digital economy, and the aerospace industry so that the people of both nations can benefit more from China-Thailand cooperation," Xi was quoted as saying by the China Central Television.

Xi added that Beijing was ready to strengthen strategic cooperation with Bangkok, in particular, within major projects such as the China-Thailand high-speed railway project, and increase imports of premium Thai agricultural products.

-- BERNAMA-SPUTNIK/RIA NOVOSTI

TAGS: China, Thailand, King Maha Vajiralongkorn visit, AI cooperation

data.title: SOBO: Q3 2025 delivered stable results and strong 2026 outlook, with $1.03B EBITDA guidance
data.description:
Q3 2025 saw stable financials with $461M revenue, $93M net income, and $254M normalized EBITDA. 2026 guidance projects $1.03B normalized EBITDA and $655M distributable cash flow, with 90% of cash flows secured. Dividend of $0.50/share declared.

Original document: South Bow Corp [SOBO] SEC 8-K Current Report — Nov. 14 2025
